releases.summary: The lambda revolution — functional programming comes to Java.
majorFeatures: Stream API for declarative data processing

jeps.title: Lambda Expressions & Virtual Extension Methods
jeps.plainEnglishSummary: Write anonymous functions inline instead of single-method anonymous classes. list.forEach(s -> System.out.println(s)) instead of a full anonymous Runnable.

releases.summary: Modularity for the JDK and your code with Project Jigsaw.
majorFeatures: Java Platform Module System (JPMS / Project Jigsaw)
Collection factory methods: List.of(), Set.of(), Map.of()

jeps.title: Module System
jeps.plainEnglishSummary: Divide the JDK and your application into modules with explicit dependencies and exported packages. Reduces classpath hell and improves security by hiding implementation packages.

releases.summary: Local variable type inference with var — less boilerplate, same safety.

jeps.title: Local-Variable Type Inference
jeps.plainEnglishSummary: Use var instead of writing out the full type for local variables. The compiler still knows the type — this is not dynamic typing. Works for local variables and for-loop variables only.

releases.summary: LTS with the new HTTP Client, String enhancements, and single-file execution.
majorFeatures: HTTP Client API (standard, replaces deprecated HttpURLConnection pattern)
String::isBlank, strip, stripLeading, stripTrailing, repeat, lines
Run single Java source files: java Hello.java without javac

jeps.title: HTTP Client
jeps.plainEnglishSummary: A modern HTTP client supporting HTTP/2, WebSocket, and async/sync modes. Replaces the clunky HttpURLConnection with a clean builder-style API.

releases.summary: Records arrive in preview; switch expressions become standard.
majorFeatures: Helpful NullPointerExceptions (NPE messages show exactly which ref was null)

jeps.title: Switch Expressions
jeps.plainEnglishSummary: Switch can now be an expression (returns a value) with the arrow syntax. No more fall-through bugs and no more forgetting break.

releases.summary: Sealed classes in preview; text blocks become standard.

jeps.title: Text Blocks
jeps.plainEnglishSummary: Multi-line string literals using triple quotes. No more string concatenation for JSON, SQL, or HTML. Indentation is automatically stripped based on the closing delimiter.

releases.summary: Records and pattern matching for instanceof graduate to standard.

jeps.title: Records
jeps.plainEnglishSummary: Concise immutable data classes. The compiler auto-generates constructor, accessors, equals, hashCode, and toString from the record components. Perfect for DTOs and value objects.

jeps.title: Pattern Matching for instanceof
jeps.plainEnglishSummary: Combine the instanceof check and cast into one operation. No more redundant cast after the check — the binding variable is available directly.

releases.summary: LTS — sealed classes standard, strong encapsulation of JDK internals enforced.
majorFeatures: Strong encapsulation of JDK internals — --illegal-access removed (JEP 403)

jeps.title: Sealed Classes
jeps.plainEnglishSummary: Restrict which classes can extend or implement a type. Enables exhaustive pattern matching and makes class hierarchies explicit. The sealed keyword + permits clause defines the closed set.

releases.summary: Virtual threads arrive in preview — Project Loom's breakthrough for scalable concurrency.

jeps.title: Virtual Threads (Preview)
jeps.plainEnglishSummary: Lightweight threads managed by the JVM rather than the OS. Create millions of them cheaply. Blocking operations (I/O, sleep) don't block the underlying OS thread — the JVM parks the virtual thread and reuses the carrier. Write blocking code that scales like async code.

releases.summary: Current LTS — virtual threads standard, pattern matching complete, sequenced collections.

jeps.title: Virtual Threads
jeps.plainEnglishSummary: Virtual threads are now production-ready. Millions of lightweight threads that block without consuming OS threads. Write simple blocking code (JDBC, HTTP calls) that scales like reactive code — no callback hell, no WebFlux, just plain Thread.sleep() and blocking I/O.

jeps.title: Record Patterns
jeps.plainEnglishSummary: Destructure record components directly in a pattern. Instead of calling accessors after a pattern match, extract the components inline. Composes with nested patterns for deep destructuring.

jeps.title: Pattern Matching for switch
jeps.plainEnglishSummary: Switch on any type (not just int/String/enum), with type patterns, guarded patterns, and exhaustiveness checking. Replaces chains of if-instanceof with readable, compiler-verified switch expressions.

jeps.title: Sequenced Collections
jeps.plainEnglishSummary: A new SequencedCollection interface gives all ordered collections (List, Deque, LinkedHashSet, LinkedHashMap) a uniform API for accessing first and last elements, and getting a reversed view. No more different idioms for different types.
